notes: BG 2.24 names nine properties — the four denials of 2.23 restated affirmatively (acchedyaḥ, adāhyaḥ, akledyaḥ, aśoṣyaḥ) plus five additional (nityaḥ, sarva-gataḥ, sthāṇuḥ, acalaḥ, sanātanaḥ). The engineering layer preserves all nine as a checklist. The four restated affirmatives are mapped distinctly (uncleavable / incombustible / unwettable / undryable). The five additions are also mapped distinctly: nitya (eternal — survives migrations), sarva-gata (all-pervading — every service must honour), sthāṇu (stable — does not drift), acala (immovable — versioning by addition not mutation), sanātana (primordial — predates implementations). Critically the engineering layer preserves the sthāṇu vs. acala distinction that English translations collapse: sthāṇu is structural-stability ('does not drift') while acala is no-spatial-movement ('versioning means adding new contracts beside it, never silent mutation'). Shankara's deductive-chain reading and Ramanuja's pervasion-by-subtlety reading are both honoured. PASS.
